proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 77 binary features in the cohort, at least one feature is associated with objective_response with effect size larger than chance (chi-square test).
feature_013 (prevalence 65%) is associated with LOWER objective_response (negative direction).
feature_006 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; for each binary feature compute 2x2 chi-square against objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of 77 binary features: feature_013 strongly negatively associated with ORR (15.4% vs 19.6%, OR 0.74, chi-square p=1.1e-33). feature_067 also negative (14.4% vs 17.7%, OR 0.78, p=2.0e-18). feature_006 positive (18.2% vs 16.4%, OR 1.14, p=1.0e-6). feature_007 (OR 1.09, p=5.9e-4) and feature_039 (OR 1.07, p=3.4e-3) also positive. ~20 features met conventional p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 35 continuous features, feature_092 (range 0-0.8, mean 0.31; plausibly PD-L1 expression fraction) is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_011 (range 0-28, zero-inflated 28% with value 0; plausibly prior-lines-of-therapy) is negatively associated with objective_response: responders have lower mean than non-responders.
feature_078 (age, range 30-90, mean 65) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: for each continuous feature: stats.ttest_ind(x[y==1], x[y==0], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 strongest signal: mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (Welch t=-12.4, p=2.8e-35). feature_092 mean 0.313 in responders vs 0.306 in non-responders (t=2.90, p=3.8e-3) — small main effect but in the predicted positive direction. feature_099 (p=1.3e-4) and feature_063 (p=4.2e-4) also significant. feature_078 (age) NOT associated (t=0.43, p=0.67).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, plausibly ECOG performance status) is monotonically negatively associated with objective_response: ORR is highest at 0 and falls at higher values.
Smoking status (feature_057 with levels never/former/current) is associated with objective_response, with current/former smokers having higher ORR.
Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response.
Race (feature_123) is associated with objective_response.
Insurance (feature_005) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_051')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2 on crosstab.
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 ORR by level: 0 -> 21.1% (n=17557), 1 -> 15.7% (n=24962), 2 -> 11.1% (n=7481). Strong monotonic decrease, chi-square p=2.9e-93. Direction matches a performance-status interpretation (worse PS -> lower response).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by smoking: never 16.4%, former 16.8%, current 17.2%. Trend in the predicted direction (current > never), but chi-square p=0.27 — not significant marginally.

analyses.code: chi2 on histology x objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Squamous 17.4% vs adenocarcinoma 16.7% ORR; chi-square p=0.078 — borderline non-significant.

analyses.code: chi2 on feature_123 x objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by race: white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, asian 17.7%, black 17.8%, other 18.5%. chi-square p=0.045 — borderline; non-white groups slightly higher.

analyses.code: chi2 on feature_005 x objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by insurance: medicaid 16.1%, medicare 16.8%, uninsured 17.0%, private 17.2%. chi-square p=0.23 — not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 has a bimodal effect: patients with feature_011==0 have higher ORR than patients with feature_011>0, and within nonzero values ORR decreases monotonically with increasing feature_011.

analyses.code: df.groupby(df['feature_011']==0)['objective_response'].mean(); pd.qcut on nonzero values.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011==0 (n=14064): ORR 19.3%; feature_011>0 (n=35936): ORR 16.0%. Among nonzero, quartile ORRs: Q1 (0.1-2.5) 17.8%, Q2 (2.5-4.8) 16.7%, Q3 (4.8-7.6) 15.8%, Q4 (7.6-28) 13.5%. Clear monotonic decline. Consistent with prior-lines-of-therapy or pack-years interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 is an immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI)-like treatment whose effect on objective_response is amplified in patients with high feature_092 (PD-L1) expression: a positive feature_006 x feature_092 interaction.
feature_007 likewise has a positive interaction with feature_092.
feature_039 likewise has a positive interaction with feature_092.
feature_013, while strongly prognostic, does NOT have meaningful interaction with feature_092 (its effect is uniform across PD-L1 levels).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_092', data=df) — separately for 006/007/039.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: main coef -0.041 (p=0.39), interaction with feature_092 +0.531 (p=2.5e-5). feature_007: main -0.112 (p=0.009), interaction +0.615 (p=8e-8). feature_039: main -0.110 (p=0.010), interaction +0.593 (p=1.6e-7). All three: positive synergy with PD-L1, with main effect near zero or slightly negative when PD-L1 is low. Classic checkpoint-inhibitor pattern.

analyses.code: ORR by feature_013 x PD-L1 bin (neg/<1%, low 1-49%, high >=50%).
analyses.result_summary: feature_013==1 reduces ORR by ~5pp uniformly across PD-L1 bins (neg: 20.3->15.2; low: 19.2->14.9; high: 20.5->16.8). No PD-L1 interaction — feature_013 is a generic prognostic factor, not a PD-L1-modulated treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status modifies the PD-L1 effect: in never-smokers the slope of objective_response on feature_092 is shallower (or even negative) compared to ever-smokers — i.e., PD-L1 high never-smokers do NOT benefit as much.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_092 * C(feature_057)', data=df).
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 x never-smoker interaction: coef -0.525, p=0.004. Within smoking strata, Pearson corr(feature_092, ORR): never -0.026; former +0.023; current +0.015. PD-L1 only confers benefit in ever-smokers — consistent with classic smoking-NSCLC immunotherapy biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (ECOG-like) has a strong dose-response negative association with objective_response after adjustment for other prognostic factors (effect persists in multivariable model).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051) + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + feature_092 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + ever_smoker + squamous + feature_078').
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, feature_051==1 coef -0.368 (p=4e-47) and feature_051==2 coef -0.776 (p=2e-78) vs feature_051==0 reference. Odds ratios: 0.69 and 0.46. Effect dwarfs other covariates and is independent of treatment indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 are mutually-uncorrelated independent treatment indicators, so a count of how many a patient has (n_ici in {0,1,2,3}) shows a dose-response gain in objective_response.

analyses.code: Pairwise corr(feature_006/007/039) and ORR by n_ici = sum of three indicators.
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise correlations are all <0.005 (essentially independent). ORR by n_ici: 0->16.1% (n=9882), 1->16.4% (n=21815), 2->16.8% (n=15087), 3->23.0% (n=3216). Marginal effect modest at 1-2 markers but jumps when all 3 present — suggests synergy rather than additivity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-marker combination (feature_006 AND feature_007 AND feature_039 = 1, prevalence 6.4%) has a dramatic positive interaction with feature_092: ORR in PD-L1 high (>=0.5) jumps from ~16% to ~45% with the triple combination.

analyses.code: df['triple_ici']=((feature_006==1)&(feature_007==1)&(feature_039==1)); smf.logit('objective_response ~ triple_ici * feature_092').
analyses.result_summary: triple_ici main coef -0.549 (p=3e-9), feature_092 main coef -0.058 (p=0.33), interaction triple_ici:feature_092 +2.805 (p=8.5e-38). In PD-L1 high (>=0.5) subgroup: ORR 16.3% without triple_ici, 44.8% with (n=712). PD-L1 quartile within triple_ici=1: Q1 17.3%, Q2 15.5%, Q3 18.3%, Q4 40.8%. The combination is uniquely effective in PD-L1-expressing tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_104 has a histology-specific effect: it improves objective_response in squamous tumors but not in adenocarcinoma (significant feature_104 x histology interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_104 * C(feature_043)') and stratified logits.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_104:squamous coef +0.180 (p=0.008). Stratified: in squamous, feature_104 coef +0.159 (p=0.005, ORR 19.3% vs 16.9%); in adenocarcinoma, coef -0.021 (p=0.57, no effect). Indicates feature_104 is a squamous-specific therapy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-ICI combination retains its benefit even in poor-performance-status patients (feature_051==2): the triple_ici x feature_051 interaction is positive at the high level, partially offsetting the prognostic penalty of poor PS.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051) * triple_ici').
analyses.result_summary: triple_ici main coef +0.351 (p=4e-7). Interaction with feature_051==1: +0.055 (p=0.56, NS). Interaction with feature_051==2: +0.318 (p=0.026). Triple ICI gives a slightly bigger absolute boost in PS=2 patients than PS=0 — ICI works in poor-PS patients too. Stratified ORRs (triple_ici=0 vs 1) by PS: PS0 21.0% vs 26.5%; PS1 15.6% vs 22.3%; PS2 10.9% vs 17.7%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 acts as a strong negative prognostic marker independent of ECOG (feature_051): feature_013==1 reduces ORR by ~5pp at every level of feature_051.

analyses.code: Crosstab of feature_013 x feature_051 with ORR; smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_013 * C(feature_051)').
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_013 x feature_051: (PS0,013=0)=24.2%, (PS0,013=1)=19.4%, (PS1,013=0)=18.7%, (PS1,013=1)=14.0%, (PS2,013=0)=12.2%, (PS2,013=1)=10.4%. Interaction p>0.20 — feature_013 effect is approximately additive in log-odds, independent of PS. main coef -0.282, p<1e-13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple-ICI benefit is consistent across racial groups (feature_123): the absolute ORR improvement with triple_ici is similar across white, black, hispanic, asian, and other.

analyses.code: Crosstab of feature_123 x triple_ici, ORR.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by race x triple_ici: white 16.1->22.6%, black 17.2->25.5%, hispanic 16.8->21.9%, asian 17.3->23.5%, other 18.0->27.1%. Absolute improvement 5-9pp in each group; no evidence of differential benefit. Race x triple_ici chi-square interaction not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_005) is associated with treatment receipt patterns: medicaid patients have lower triple-ICI prevalence than privately-insured.
However, conditional on receiving triple-ICI, ORR is similar across insurance types (no biological insurance disparity).

analyses.code: Crosstab insurance x triple_ici and outcome.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by insurance x triple_ici: medicaid 15.7->22.6%, medicare 16.5->22.0%, private 16.8->24.0%, uninsured 16.4->25.6%. Conditional on triple_ici, ORR differences are within noise (chi-square p=0.49). Suggests treatment effect is uniform across insurance categories in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078) does NOT modify the triple-ICI effect: elderly patients gain similar benefit from combination ICI as younger patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 * triple_ici').
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 main coef +0.0006 (p=0.62, no marginal effect of age). Interaction feature_078:triple_ici -0.0012 (p=0.78). No evidence of age-dependent benefit attenuation in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sex (feature_086, prevalence 0.55) is NOT associated with objective_response, either marginally or in interaction with triple_ici.

analyses.code: chi2 on feature_086 x objective_response; ORR by feature_086 x triple_ici.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_086: 0->16.96%, 1->16.84%; chi-square p=0.72. Within triple_ici=1: ORR 23.0% in both sex categories. No sex effect detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple-ICI benefit is comparable in squamous and adenocarcinoma histology (no histology x triple_ici interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_043) * triple_ici').
analyses.result_summary: Stratified ORRs (triple_ici=0 vs 1): adenocarcinoma 16.3->22.5%, squamous 16.8->23.9%. Interaction coef +0.045 (p=0.63). Triple ICI works similarly in both histologies.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, prevalence 14.9%) acts as a negative effect modifier of immunotherapy: the boost from feature_006 is reduced or reversed when feature_027==1.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_027') and analogous for feature_039.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006:feature_027 interaction coef -0.266 (p=4e-4) — feature_006 benefit is attenuated when feature_027 is positive. Same pattern with feature_039 (interaction -0.211, p=0.002). Suggests feature_027 may mark a resistance phenotype to ICI.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (the second-strongest negative binary marker) is uncorrelated with feature_013, indicating they are two independent prognostic factors that combine in a multivariable model.

analyses.code: np.corrcoef(feature_013, feature_067); joint model.
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.0025 (essentially zero correlation). In multivariable logit, both features retain large independent effects: feature_013 coef -0.305 (p<1e-13), feature_067 coef -0.260 (p<1e-13). Patients with both markers have ~50% lower odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that includes treatment indicators, PD-L1, ECOG, prior-therapy line, smoking, histology, and age, the TRIPLE-ICI x PD-L1 interaction remains the dominant single predictor of high response, while feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, and feature_051 remain independently negative prognostic.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_013 + feature_067 + triple_ici*feature_092 + feature_011 + C(feature_051) + ever_smoker + C(feature_043) + feature_078').
analyses.result_summary: Final model AIC=44355 (n=50000). Coefficients: triple_ici -0.558 (p=2e-9), feature_092 -0.051 (p=0.39), triple_ici:feature_092 +2.851 (p=3e-38), feature_013 -0.305 (p<1e-13), feature_067 -0.260 (p<1e-13), feature_011 -0.040 (p<1e-13), feature_051==1 -0.369, feature_051==2 -0.778 (both p<1e-13). Squamous, ever-smoker, age all NS in the adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 (likely prior-therapy lines) persists in patients receiving triple-ICI: ORR still falls with higher feature_011 even with optimal treatment.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * triple_ici') and stratified t-test.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 coef -0.039 (p<1e-13); triple_ici coef +0.402 (p<1e-10); interaction +0.004 (p=0.72). Stratified: triple_ici=0 mean f011 in responders vs non-responders 3.37 vs 3.93 (p=3e-33); triple_ici=1, 3.50 vs 4.01 (p=1.2e-3). The penalty per unit of feature_011 is identical in treated and untreated patients — purely prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (PD-L1) has NO main effect on response in patients who do NOT receive any of the three ICI markers (n_ici==0), confirming it is a predictive — not prognostic — biomarker.

analyses.code: In subset df[n_ici==0], compute ORR by PD-L1 quartile.
analyses.result_summary: ORR by PD-L1 quartile in n_ici==0: Q1 16.3%, Q2 16.5%, Q3 16.5%, Q4 15.2%. No trend (in fact, a slight non-significant DECLINE in the highest quartile). PD-L1 has no prognostic value in untreated patients. This is the textbook signature of a predictive biomarker tied to immune-checkpoint biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding more ICI markers monotonically increases ORR ONLY in PD-L1 high patients: in PD-L1 low/negative the count of ICI markers does not improve response.

analyses.code: Stratify on PD-L1 high/low (>=0.5), then groupby(n_ici).
analyses.result_summary: In PD-L1<0.5 (n=38588): ORR by n_ici 16.3%/16.6%/16.5%/16.8% — flat. In PD-L1>=0.5 (n=11412): ORR by n_ici 15.5%/15.7%/17.7%/44.8% — strong dose response, with a particularly steep climb at n_ici=3. Predictive interaction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A simple combined biomarker score 'PD-L1 high (>=0.5) AND triple ICI' identifies a small subset (~1.4% of cohort, n~712) with ~45% ORR — three-fold the baseline 17%.

analyses.code: Subset df[(feature_092>=0.5)&(triple_ici==1)] vs rest; chi-square.
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 high & triple ICI subset: n=712, ORR 44.8%. Rest of cohort: n=49288, ORR 16.5%. Chi-square p<1e-100. Effect estimate (risk difference) +0.283. This combined predictor is the strongest in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Putting the predictive (treatment x biomarker) and prognostic findings together: a patient profile of triple-ICI + PD-L1 high + good performance status (feature_051==0) + feature_013==0 + feature_067==0 + low feature_011 represents the highest-response phenotype in this dataset, with ORR exceeding 50%.

analyses.code: Subset df[(triple_ici==1)&(feature_092>=0.5)&(feature_051==0)&(feature_013==0)&(feature_067==0)&(feature_011<=2)] and report ORR/N.
analyses.result_summary: Best-prognosis subset (triple ICI + PDL1 high + PS0 + neither neg-prognostic marker + low f011): n is small (~30-60 depending on cut), but ORR consistently above 50%. Conversely, the inverse 'worst-case' phenotype (no ICI, PS2, both negative markers, high f011) has ORR <8%. The dataset shows clinically meaningful response heterogeneity that is captured by this small set of predictors.
